title: Translations of the library (per-language, reviewer-gated)
context: Reviewer-gated translation of the dual-signed ewing-survivorship-late-effects library into additional languages (a representative, per-language task; the language set is open and not yet enumerated). Translation is a writing deliverable of type writing; each language is HIGH risk-tier and gated by a qualified clinical AND advocate reviewer for that language, preserving the no-advice label and fact-vs-expression copyright firewall (synthesize facts and cite; never reproduce protected guideline text/tables/figures). Only after M6; no language is fabricated here - concrete languages expand on reviewer/partner confirmation. As a translation of our own CC-BY-4.0 content, the translation inherits CC-BY-4.0 (never relicense copyrighted source).
objective: Produce a reviewer-gated translation of the published library for one confirmed target language, preserving sourcing, labels, and the dual-review gate.
acceptanceCriteria: Translation preserves every sourceRef, the no-advice label, exposure-keying, and seek-care routing of the source content
Each language gated by a qualified clinical AND advocate reviewer fluent in that language; neither alone permits publish
Reading-level and accessibility targets met in the target language; copyright firewall upheld; license remains source-compatible (CC-BY-4.0)
Only undertaken after M6; concrete languages added only on reviewer/partner confirmation (none fabricated)
